title: Manufacturing Process Scientist
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**

**How will you make an impact?**


Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. is the world leader in serving science, with annual revenue of more than $40 billion. Our Mission is to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. Whether our customers are accelerating life sciences research, solving complex analytical challenges, improving laboratory efficiency, advancing patient health through diagnostics, or developing and manufacturing life\-changing therapies, we are here to support them.


We are seeking a highly motivated **Manufacturing Process Scientist** to join the Pharmaceutical Development Services (PDS) team in Monza, Italy.


The Pharmaceutical Development Services (PDS) division specializes in clinical and small\-scale commercial contract manufacturing of sterile injectable drug products, including liquid and lyophilized formulations.


You will join the **Process Manufacturing Sciences** team, where scientists design, develop and optimize manufacturing processes for innovative therapies, working in close partnership with clients and cross\-functional teams. This role offers the opportunity to contribute to the successful delivery of life\-changing medicines while developing your scientific expertise in a dynamic and collaborative environment.

**What will you do?**


As a **Manufacturing Process Scientist**, you will support the development, optimization and technology transfer of sterile manufacturing processes into the cGMP manufacturing suites at the Monza site.


As the **Process Subject Matter Expert (SME)** for assigned projects, you will provide scientific leadership throughout the project lifecycle, partnering with clients and cross\-functional teams to develop robust, scalable and compliant manufacturing processes for clinical and commercial products.

**Your responsibilities will include:**

* Define, develop and optimize manufacturing processes for sterile injectable products based on product knowledge, development data and Quality Target Product Profile (QTPP).
* Prepare and review technical documentation, including manufacturing instructions, study protocols, technical reports, FMEA and GAP analyses.
* Drive the technical execution of assigned projects, supporting process development, scale\-up, technology transfer and manufacturing activities.
* Build strong scientific partnerships with clients, provide technical expertise and supporting process\-related decisions throughout the project lifecycle.
* Lead technical investigations, risk assessments and process optimization to ensure robust and compliant manufacturing processes.
* Collaborate with Manufacturing, MSAT, Quality, Engineering and other cross\-functional teams to ensure successful project execution.
* Present scientific data, project progress and technical recommendations to internal stakeholders and external clients.
* Support client meetings, audits and regulatory inspections as the process technical expert.
* Stay current with emerging technologies and scientific literature to drive innovation and continuous improvement.

**Qualifications**

**Minimum Qualifications**

* Bachelor's or Master's degree in Pharmacy, Pharmaceutical Chemistry and Technology, Biotechnology, Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, or another relevant scientific discipline.
* 2\-3 years of experience in sterile pharmaceutical process development and/or manufacturing is a must.
* 2\-3 years of experience with liquid, suspension and/or lyophilized drug products is also a must.
* Good knowledge of GMP requirements; experience with GLP is considered an advantage.
* 2\-3 years of experience preparing technical documentation such as manufacturing instructions, study protocols, technical reports, FMEA and GAP analyses is a must.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.

**Preferred Competencies**

* Strong scientific mindset with excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills.
* Ability to manage multiple projects and priorities in a fast\-paced environment.
* Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to interact effectively with clients and cross\-functional teams.
* High attention to detail and commitment to quality.
* Curiosity, accountability and a continuous learning mindset.
* Ability to work both independently and collaboratively within multidisciplinary teams.

**Join us!**


At Thermo Fisher Scientific, each one of our more than 100,000 extraordinary minds has a unique story to tell. Join us and contribute to our singular mission—enabling our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer.
company_description: At Thermo Fisher Scientific, our professionals develop critical solutions—and build rewarding careers.

title: LIFE SCIENCES SOLUTIONS ENGINEER
description:
Location: Milano \- Italy
Department: Others
**About Philogen**



Philogen S.p.A. is a biotechnology company dedicated to developing targeted products for the imaging and treatment of life\-threatening diseases, with a strategic focus on oncology. Our mission is to translate cutting\-edge scientific innovation into effective therapies that improve patients’ lives.


**Position Overview**



We are looking for a Life Sciences Solutions Engineer who combines strong coding skills with deep expertise in at least one of the following areas:


* Pharmaceutical quality systems or quality control.
* GxP, computerized\-system compliance, or regulated software validation.
* Clinical trial operations, clinical data management, or trial oversight.


This is not a documentation\-only compliance role. You will inspect data, write code, configure and test systems, and work directly with engineers, clinical teams, quality specialists, and business stakeholders. You will guide the introduction of production\-grade AI and data systems into regulated workflows.


**What You Will Do**


* Translate pharmaceutical and clinical requirements into implementable data models, workflows and access rules.
* Guide software deployment through the applicable quality, privacy, security, and regulatory requirements.
* Determine which regulatory frameworks apply to each use case rather than treating every healthcare system as subject to identical controls.
* Analyze clinical trial data covering studies, sites, subjects, visits, forms, observations, queries, reviews, medical coding, and audit history.
* Define and test controls for sensitive information, including role\-based access, separation of duties, approvals, and audit trails.
* Produce and maintain documentation such as intended\-use definitions, risk assessments, traceability matrices, validation plans, test evidence, operating procedures, and change records.
* Work with quality, clinical, security, legal, and engineering stakeholders to resolve differences between written procedures and actual system behavior.
* Support technical workshops, deployment planning, validation, training, and post\-deployment reviews.


**What We Are Looking For**


* 5\+ years of professional experience in software engineering, data engineering, solutions architecture, technical consulting, or a comparable hands\-on technical role.
* Deep professional expertise in at least one of pharmaceutical quality, regulated computerized systems, or clinical trials.
* Strong coding skills, preferably in Python, together with practical SQL and data\-modeling experience.
* Ability to understand complex data pipelines and trace how a source record is transformed, connected, exposed, and audited.
* Ability to communicate effectively with software engineers and specialists from quality, clinical operations, data management, and compliance.
* Experience in a pharmaceutical, biotechnology, healthcare, CRO, technology consultancy, or regulated cloud/software environment.
* Strong professional English and the ability to produce precise technical and validation documentation.
* High daily engagement with modern AI tools and an interest in the responsible deployment of agent\-based systems.


**Relevant Areas of Expertise**



Candidates are not expected to know every framework below, but should have substantial practical experience in those relevant to their previous work:


* ICH Good Clinical Practice and clinical trial data governance.
* GxP and pharmaceutical quality systems.
* Computerized\-system validation and risk\-based Computer Software Assurance.
* EU Annex 11 and FDA 21 CFR Part 11\.
* GDPR and health\-data access controls.
* ALCOA\+ data\-integrity principles.
* Clinical data management, electronic data capture, source\-data review, clinical queries, coding, and trial audit trails.
* EU medical\-device or software regulation where applicable to a system’s intended use.
* Quality risk management, change control, deviation management, CAPA, and supplier qualification.


**Nice to Have**


* Experience delivering infrastructure, data, or AI platforms for pharmaceutical customers.
* Familiarity with clinical data standards such as CDISC ODM, SDTM, or ADaM.
* Experience with electronic data\-capture systems, clinical trial management systems, quality\-management systems, laboratory systems, or regulatory document platforms.
* Knowledge of cloud security, Kubernetes, identity management, RBAC, and infrastructure\-as\-code.
* Experience with knowledge graphs, ontologies, AI agents, retrieval systems, or governed generative\-AI deployments.
* Experience participating in audits, inspections, validation reviews, or regulated customer\-qualification processes.
* A professional background as a software engineer, data engineer, or solutions architect before specializing in life sciences.


**Education**


* Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, Life Sciences, Biotechnology, Pharmaceutical Sciences, or a related field.
* A mixed technical and life\-sciences background is strongly preferred.
* Strong academic credentials with international exposure.


**What We Offer**


* Full\-time employment under the Chemical and Pharmaceutical Industry CCNL.
* Competitive gross annual salary ranging from €70,000 to €110,000 RAL. Positioning within the salary range will be determined based on objective and neutral criteria, taking into account the candidate’s skills, experience, and the level of responsibility associated with the role, as assessed throughout the selection process.
* A hands\-on role at the intersection of software engineering, clinical operations, and pharmaceutical compliance.
* The opportunity to define how AI\-enabled systems are validated and introduced into regulated workflows.
* Direct exposure to leadership and high\-impact projects across Philogen.
* The opportunity to contribute to applied AI across both operational and scientific workflows.
* A fast\-moving, engineering\-driven environment focused on real delivery rather than prototypes.


**Job Location**



Milan, Italy.


*We invite interested candidates to submit their applications expressly authorizing the processing of personal data in accordance with Legislative Decree 196/2003\. Our recruitment adheres to the provisions of Legislative Decree 198/2006, and we welcome applications from candidates of any gender orientation or expression, sexual orientation, age, ethnicity, or religious belief.*  

*In compliance with Directive (EU) 2023/970 and Italian Legislative Decree No. 96 of 7 May 2026, this job advertisement has been drafted using gender\-neutral criteria.*

title: Specification Engineer - Emergency Lighting Key Accounts
description:
Within Eaton’s Electrical business, the **Life Safety Division (LSD)** plays a critical role in protecting people and buildings during emergency situations. The division is globally recognized for its expertise in **emergency lighting** , fire detection, alarm, notification, and mass notification systems—enabling safe evacuation and supporting regulatory compliance across demanding commercial and institutional environments.


To support our continued growth, we are seeking a **Specification Engineer – Emergency Lighting Key Accounts** for the Lombardy and Northern Italy region.


In this strategic role, the Specification Engineer – Emergency Lighting Key Accounts is responsible for developing and strengthening a robust network of key specification stakeholders within an assigned territory, with a particular focus on **emergency lighting solutions** for commercial and institutional building projects. These stakeholders typically include engineering consultancies, design authorities, and major end‑users managing standardized portfolios or high‑value developments.

\#LI\-EC1


Eaton provides a competitive total rewards package based on applicable skills, knowledge and experience, and aligned with internal and external market practices. The annual salary range (including base salary and Sales Incentive Plan) for this role is EUR 55\.200– EUR 80\.960 gross per year.


All positions may require participation in video and in\-person interviews as part of the hiring process. All candidates will be evaluated based on job\-related competencies, and all candidates’ privacy rights and data security will be protected in accordance with applicable laws.


We are committed to ensuring equal employment opportunities for job applicants and employees. Our recruitment processes use balanced selection criteria and avoid unlawful discrimination against applicants on the basis of their age, colour, disability, marital status, national origin, gender, gender identity, genetic information, race or racial origin, religion, sexual orientation or any other status protected or required by law.

**Job Description Summary:**
----------------------------

Vector Renewables is looking for a highly motivated trainee in the renewable energy industry, to be joining the Technical Advisory team in Italy.
  

  

The Italy Technical Advisory team supports investors, lenders, developers and asset owners across the full lifecycle of renewable energy projects, providing technical advisory services for wind, solar PV and battery energy storage systems (BESS). Our work spans transaction\-related assignments, such as Technical Due Diligence for M\&A and Project Finance processes, as well as technical assessments for projects under development, repowering and operation.
**Job Description:**
--------------------

As part of the Italy Technical Advisory team, the successful candidate will support the delivery of independent technical advisory services for utility\-scale renewable energy projects. Working under the guidance of experienced engineers and technical advisors, the candidate will contribute to projects at different stages of their lifecycle, from development and financing through construction and operation. The role will provide exposure to multidisciplinary engineering activities, project finance transactions and technical assessments for international clients.


Typical responsibilities and activities will include:


* Supporting the review of technical documentation, including project design packages, engineering reports, permits and contractual documentation.
* Assisting in Technical Due Diligence assignments for Project Finance and M\&A transactions by reviewing project documentation, identifying technical risks and preparing technical summaries.
* Supporting energy yield assessments through the review of wind and solar resource data, production estimates and associated technical assumptions.
* Assisting in the technical review of civil, electrical and mechanical project design, verifying compliance with applicable standards, industry best practices and employer's requirements.
* Supporting the assessment of renewable energy technologies, including wind turbines, PV modules, inverters, transformers and battery energy storage systems.
* Contributing to the review of grid connection solutions, construction progress, commissioning documentation and operational performance, where applicable.
* Performing engineering calculations, technical analyses and desktop studies under the supervision of senior engineers.
* Preparing technical reports, presentations and client deliverables in both Italian and English.
* Carrying out technical research on emerging technologies, regulatory developments and market trends affecting the renewable energy sector.
* Collaborating with multidisciplinary teams across Vector Renewables' international offices to support the successful delivery of advisory projects.

The position will be based in our Milan office and will mainly involve desktop analysis and technical reporting for customers and collaboration within multidisciplinary project teams.


**Requested qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree in engineering. MSc Degree will be considered a plus.
* Fluency in Italian and English language, additional languages spoken are considered as a plus.
* Excellent written and verbal communications skills, including the ability to write clear and detailed technical reports, presentations, and emails both in Italian and English.
* Good analytical skills, attention to detail and a proactive attitude towards problem solving.
* Strong interest in the renewable energy sector and motivation to develop a career in technical advisory.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, and Outlook.
* Valid driving license and availability to travel occasionally within Italy, if required.

**Desired qualifications:**

* Charter Engineer qualifications will be considered a plus.
* Electrical background or familiarity with grid\-related aspects of renewable energy projects will be considered a plus.
* Mechanical background with technology\-related aspects of renewable energy projects will be considered a plus.
* Technical knowledge of Wind, PV and BESS projects will be considered a plus.
* Familiarity with Technical Due Diligence or broader technical advisory activities in the renewable energy sector.
* Knowledge and interest in data centre market will be considered a plus.
* Interest in the development of hybrid renewable projects and energy storage applications
* Knowledge of specialized software, especially WindPro, WindSim, WAsP and PVsyst.
* Perform energy yield assessments of wind and PV projects will be considered a plus.

**What do we expect from you?**

We are looking for someone with curiosity, commitment, strong willingness to learn and ability to frame technical work within the broader project, transaction and stakeholders context. The role requires a collaborative mindset, a solid technical attitude and the ability to work across different technologies and project stages in a fast\-moving advisory environment.


At Vector Renewables, you will have the opportunity to work on a wide range of renewable energy projects and to develop your skills alongside experienced professionals in a growing international technical advisory business. We always look for enablers of growth!


**Location:**

Milano \- Martesana

Full time
**Worker Subtype:**

Intern (Fixed Term)

**Job Application Safety Reminder ️**


We’re seeing an increase in fraudulent job offers. To protect yourself, please follow these key guidelines when applying for roles at ING:


* Apply only via official ING platforms: ING uses Workday as its internal recruitment system. Applications should be submitted only via our official career site.


* Check the sender’s email carefully: legitimate communication will always come from: @ing.com and/or @myworkday.com


* No payments or banking details will ever be requested. If someone asks for this information, it’s a scam.


If you suspect suspicious activity, report it immediately. Your safety matters to us.

The Environment:

Our Milan office is located in a well\-connected commercial district near Porta Nuova and Milano Centrale. You will work in a professional and collaborative environment with engineers, project specialists and technical consultants.

Meaningful Projects:

You will contribute to electrical infrastructure, industrial systems, renewable energy and energy\-efficiency projects that have a direct impact on clients and communities.

Technical Responsibility:

You will have the authority to influence design standards, engineering methods and technical decisions from the early planning stage through construction and commissioning.

Professional Growth:

We support continuous development through technical training, industry certifications and participation in relevant engineering conferences and professional events.

Collaborative Culture:

You will work with experienced professionals who value clear communication, practical solutions and high engineering standards.

Key Responsibilities:

Electrical Design Leadership:

Lead the preparation of electrical design packages for commercial, industrial and energy projects.

Technical Calculations:

Perform and review load calculations, cable sizing, voltage\-drop analysis, short\-circuit studies and equipment selection.

Design Documentation:

Prepare and approve single\-line diagrams, schematics, layouts, technical specifications, bills of materials and construction drawings.

Project Coordination:

Coordinate electrical requirements with mechanical, civil, structural and automation engineering teams.

Technical Review:

Review contractor drawings, supplier submissions and engineering documents to ensure compliance with project requirements.

Standards and Compliance:

Ensure that all designs comply with applicable Italian regulations, European standards, safety requirements and client specifications.

Team Guidance:

Provide technical support and mentoring to junior and mid\-level electrical engineers and designers.

Client Communication:

Present technical solutions, design recommendations and project updates to clients, consultants and internal stakeholders.

Site Support:

Assist with technical inspections, installation reviews, testing, commissioning and resolution of site\-related engineering issues.

Quality Control:

Maintain high standards of accuracy, documentation quality and design consistency across assigned projects.

What We Are Looking For:

Experience:

At least 6 years of experience in electrical engineering or electrical systems design, including experience coordinating complex projects or supporting other engineers.

Education:

Bachelor's or Master's degree in Electrical Engineering, Energy Engineering or a related technical discipline.

Technical Knowledge:

Strong knowledge of low\-voltage and medium\-voltage systems, power distribution, protection systems, electrical equipment and industrial installations.

Software Skills:

Proficiency in AutoCAD, electrical design software and calculation tools. Experience with Revit MEP, ETAP, EPLAN or similar platforms is an advantage.

Regulatory Knowledge:

Good understanding of Italian and European electrical regulations, including relevant CEI and IEC standards.

Leadership Skills:

Ability to review technical work, provide constructive guidance and coordinate engineering activities across several project teams.

Problem\-Solving:

Strong analytical skills with the ability to identify technical risks and develop practical engineering solutions.

Communication Skills:

Professional proficiency in Italian is required. Good working knowledge of English is preferred for technical documentation and international project coordination.

Mindset:

You are organised, detail\-oriented and commercially aware. You understand when additional analysis is necessary and when a practical, compliant solution is the best approach.

Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent

Pay: €69,500\.00 \- €110,000\.00 per year

Work Location: In person

title: Global Mobility Business Partner
description:
At **ABB**, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

**This position reports to:**


GBS HR Expert Services Global Mobility M
\_\_

**Mission:**


Serve as strategic business partner and trusted advisor to the Division, developing mobility solutions that align with business strategy. Lead specialized mobility team in delivering exceptional support for complex assignments, building organizational capability through education and training, and creating innovative programs that maximize GM as a business resource and partner.

**Key responsibilities:**

**Strategic Business Partnership:** Act as primary mobility advisor to Division leadership. Develop deep understanding of Division business strategy, talent needs, and organizational challenges. Provide strategic counsel on how mobility can support business objectives and talent strategy. Anticipate future mobility needs and proactively develop solutions.

**Team Leadership \& Case Allocation:** Lead and mentor specialized mobility team in delivering complex assignment support. Allocate cases strategically based on complexity, team expertise, and development opportunities. Provide coaching and guidance on challenging cases, serving as escalation point for complex or sensitive matters. Build team capability through feedback and development.

**Complex Case Advisory:** Serve as sparring partner for Division business leaders on complex, high\-stakes international assignments. Provide specialized guidance on policy interpretation, exception handling, and unique business scenarios. Lead problem\-solving on multi\-country assignments, third\-country nationals, and specialized talent movements.

**Business Education \& Training:** Design and deliver training programs to build Division management and HR capability on mobility strategy, policy, and best practices. Develop educational content for business leaders on talent development through international assignments. Drive awareness of mobility as a strategic tool for succession planning and leadership development.

**Program Creation \& Management:** Develop and manage Division\-specific mobility programs aligned with business strategy (e.g., leadership development programs, talent exchange initiatives, emerging market assignments). Customize policies and processes to address unique Division needs. Launch and oversee special initiatives that enhance mobility effectiveness.

**Compliance \& Risk Management:** Ensure legal and regulatory compliance across all mobility matters. Collaborate with security and legal teams to mitigate risks for assignees and the organization. Maintain health and safety standards for international assignments.

**Organizational Capability Building:** Lead knowledge\-sharing initiatives across the Division. Capture and share mobility best practices and lessons learned. Build a culture of continuous improvement in how mobility supports talent and business objectives.

**Key requirements:**


To be successful in this role, you bring at least 10 years of experience in global mobility, along with a track record of shaping mobility strategies that create value for both the business and its people. You have established yourself as a trusted strategic advisor who leads change with confidence, influencing senior business leaders and partnering effectively across a global organization.


Your expertise spans the full spectrum of international mobility, including immigration, tax, compensation, and policy design. You combine this technical knowledge with strong commercial awareness, enabling you to align mobility solutions with evolving business priorities and the needs of different divisions.


You thrive in complex, international environments where collaboration and influence matter as much as expertise. Whether you are guiding senior stakeholders, navigating organizational change, or solving challenging mobility issues, you bring a strategic mindset, sound judgment, and the ability to build trust at every level.


You are passionate about developing others and have experience designing and delivering engaging training programs that strengthen capabilities across teams. As a leader and mentor, you enjoy helping people grow while fostering a culture of continuous learning and collaboration.


Your analytical thinking allows you to translate data into meaningful insights, while your innovative approach helps you identify opportunities to improve processes and deliver lasting impact. With a strong international perspective and high cultural awareness, you are comfortable working across borders and collaborating with diverse stakeholders around the world.


Finally, you communicate with clarity and confidence in English, both spoken and written. Additional language skills are an advantage and further support your ability to build strong relationships in a global environment.


Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences.




Ready to make an impact?


Apply today or visit **https://www.abb.com** to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

title: Euronext Securities- Compliance Officer
description:
**Job title**

Compliance officer (middle level)


**Department**

Euronext Securities Milan Compliance


**Reporting**

Reporting to the Head of CSDs Compliance and Euronext Securities Milan Chief Compliance Officer


S tart gross salary from 40\.000€


**Tasks and Responsibility**

* Support and proactive collaboration with the local Chief Compliance Officer in the main compliance activities.
* Collaboration in the monitoring relevant legislation, regulations and directives framework (e.g. CSDR, GDPR, AML) applicable to the Company.
* Conduct compliance risk assessments, gap analyses and other compliance analyses to identify and mitigate compliance risks within the central securities depository business.
* Prepare, update and control internal governing documents such as policies and procedures, ensuring alignment with external and group requirements.
* Execute compliance\-related internal controls and thematic reviews
* Preparing regulatory and management reporting, including preparing and presenting compliance reports to senior management and other internal stakeholders.
* Support business units with advice on Euronext Securities activities and maintain continuous dialogue with employees across the organisation regarding compliance\-related topics.
* Coordinate and cooperate with other compliance teams and stakeholders to ensure a harmonised approach.
* Develop presentations and training material and conduct training in accordance with the compliance plan to promote compliance awareness.
* Contribution to the drafting and update of the annual compliance plan and follow up on executed compliance activities.
* Identify and implement activities to develop and mature the compliance culture across the CSDs and business units.
* Contribute to compliance\-related projects as relevant.
* Perform various administration and planning tasks within the compliance area.
* Manage ad hoc tasks as required.

**Qualifications**

* University degree in law, finance, business administration or a related field.
* At least five/six years of experience in compliance, risk management or a related area, preferably within financial services
* Knowledge of relevant legislation, regulations and directives applicable to the financial industry
* Experience in conducting compliance risk assessments, gap analyses and internal controls.
* Proven ability to prepare, update and implement compliance policies, procedures and governing documents.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to advise and support business units and maintain relationships with internal and external stakeholders.
* Strong organisational and planning skills, with the ability to proactively manage multiple tasks and projects independently.
* High level of integrity, attention to detail and commitment to maintaining a strong compliance culture.
* Proficiency in English (spoken and written); additional languages are an advantage.

W e are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate against individuals on the basis of race, gender, age, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, or any other legally protected factor. We value the unique talents of all our people, who come from diverse backgrounds with different personal experiences and points of view and we are committed to providing an environment of mutual respect.

title: Boutique Operations Specialist
description:
Richemont, one of the world leaders in the luxury sector, has various Houses specializing in jewelry, watches and high\-end accessories. Each Maison proudly embodies a tradition of style, quality and craftsmanship and Richemont strives to preserve the heritage and identity specific to each of them. At the same time, we are committed to innovating and designing new products in line with the values of our Houses, through a process of permanent creativity.


YOUR MISSION:


As an Ambassador of the Maison, this role is pivotal in ensuring the seamless and efficient execution of daily operational activities. The individual will contribute significantly to optimal back\-office performance and maintain organizational security, collaborating closely with the Manager and various departments to uphold the Maison's high standards.
  

  

HOW WILL YOU MAKE AN IMPACT?


**Boutique Operational Excellence:**

· Ensure strict adherence to all brand and boutique operational policies and procedures.


· Actively participate in boutique opening and closing procedures, including setup and breakdown activities.


· Manage and maintain optimal stock levels for all boutique facilities, including packaging, office supplies, and general consumables, initiating orders and addressing shortages proactively.


· Execute timely replenishment of proximity stock on the sales floor to support continuous sales operations.


· Act as a primary point of contact for IT\-related issues within the boutique, liaising effectively with the central IT department to ensure prompt resolution and minimal operational disruption.  



**Back\-Office \& Administrative Support:**

· Provide essential support to back\-office staff in the reception, processing, and loading of goods.


· Maintain and update records, ensuring the accuracy and integrity of all information.


· Organize and manage the secure archiving of confidential files and documents.


· Perform professional receptionist duties, managing communications and visitor interactions effectively.  



**Inventory \& Data Management:**

· Participate actively in all inventory counts and contribute to stock reconciliation processes.


· Accurately perform price labeling for merchandise.  



  

  

HOW WILL YOU EXPERIENCE SUCCESS WITH US?


We are looking for candidates with previous experience hospitality sector or in luxury Retail with a focus on operations; good interpersonal and communication skills are required as well strong attention to detail and customer needs. Team\-Player approach and Fluent in English and Italian.


CCNL Commercio


Salary Range 30K – 35KEUR


Commission Scheme


8 EUR Meal Voucher per working day


Welfare


\#Richemont \#WeCraftTheFuture
